decks.notes: The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly deploy high-impact creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter while backing them up with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain tempo throughout the game by using cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration to filter through the deck and find the necessary threats and answers. With a focus on efficiency and aggression, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent and close out the game before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on rapidly filling the graveyard with cards to bring out powerful creatures like Hollow One and Detective's Phoenix. Burning Inquiry helps with card draw while also filling the graveyard. Marauding Mako and Nethergoyf provide additional aggressive threats. Lightning Bolt and Fatal Push offer removal options for dealing with opponent threats. The mana base includes Blackcleave Cliffs and fetch lands to ensure consistent mana access. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced aggressive strategy fueled by graveyard interactions and efficient creatures.

The deck focuses on aggressively attacking the opponent with creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to deal damage. It also includes cards like Thraben Charm and Voice of Victory for additional control and protection. The mana base includes lands that can provide different colors, and cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer can generate additional value. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a mix of creatures, burn spells, and control elements.

The deck focuses on assembling a variety of creatures with different abilities to create a synergistic toolbox for combo plays. It utilizes cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician, Agatha's Soul Cauldron, and Walking Ballista to control the board and generate value. The deck also includes cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Spymaster's Vault to tutor for specific creatures when needed. By utilizing a combination of creature abilities and efficient tutoring, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents to achieve victory in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deploy artifacts and creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga, Mishra's Bauble, and Emry, Lurker of the Loch, the deck aims to create a board presence that is difficult for the opponent to deal with. This strategy is supported by cards like Pithing Needle and Tormod's Crypt to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of Arcbound Ravager and Engineered Explosives further enhances the deck's ability to control the battlefield and push for a quick win.

The main concept of this deck is to use land destruction, removal spells, and control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up your own board presence. The deck focuses on denying the opponent resources through destroying their lands and creatures, while also keeping their own board safe with removal spells and counters. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds a tribal element to the deck and provides additional value to your creatures. Overall, the deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent in the long run.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures through various means like cycling cards and using discard effects. Once enough creatures are in the graveyard, the player can cast Living End to bring all their creatures back onto the battlefield, overwhelming the opponent with a sudden surge of powerful creatures and potentially winning the game in one big swing. The deck also includes a mix of disruptive elements like Force of Negation and Subtlety to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's game plan by using counterspells, removal, and card draw. It aims to maintain board control while disrupting the opponent's strategy through cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck also has various ways to search for specific answers and generate card advantage. By controlling the game tempo and out-valuing the opponent, it looks to grind out wins in the long game. Additionally, the deck utilizes a mix of removal and counterspells to deal with threats efficiently while slowly advancing its own win condition.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End. Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker provide additional threats while Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command help control the board. The combo potential lies in using Devourer of Destiny and Blade of the Bloodchief to pump up a creature's power and toughness to quickly take down opponents. The deck also includes some utility cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Ugin's Labyrinth for additional support and disruption.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by direct damage spells such as Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck also includes cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the use of Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison adds additional versatility and control elements to the deck, allowing for different lines of play depending on the matchup.

This deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard to the battlefield. It also includes cards like Ephemerate and Solitude for protection, while utilizing cards like Faithful Mending and Thoughtseize for card advantage and disruption. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Ulamog, the Defiler and Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithless Looting. It also includes disruption through cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and protect the combo. Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides additional value and helps to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to assemble the reanimation combo quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats, utilizing instant speed plays to keep the opponent off balance.

This deck revolves around using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard. Cards like Ephemerate and Faithful Mending help with card draw and recursion, while Force of Negation provides protection. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Prismatic Ending to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and disruptive plays.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to consistently put pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cheap spells like Lightning Bolt and Mutagenic Growth, along with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total while also disrupting their game plan. Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration help to filter through the deck and find the necessary cards to maintain the aggressive strategy. The mana base is designed to support casting both red and blue spells efficiently.

The key concept of this deck is to utilize the interactions between cards like Blade of the Bloodchief, Basking Broodscale, and Kozilek's Command to create powerful combos and generate value. By utilizing cards such as Urza's Saga and Ancient Stirrings to search for key pieces, the deck aims to control the game while setting up its win condition with creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Malevolent Rumble and Unholy Heat allows the deck to disrupt opponents' strategies while maintaining its own game plan of overwhelming the opponent with powerful synergies.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay, utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to pressure the opponent. Cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Voice of Victory provide additional value and card advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's game plan. Ranger-Captain of Eos and Goblin Bombardment offer versatile options for dealing with threats. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes fetch lands to thin the deck and fix mana. Overall, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creatures and disruption.
